document.title: Standard for Unshelled Pistachio Nuts
document.label: CXS 131-1981 - Standard for Unshelled Pistachio Nuts

commodities.name: unshelled pistachio nuts
terms: unshelled pistachio packed in bulk containers

sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: This standard applies to unshelled pistachios from varieties of Pistacia vera L. either in natural or in processed condition and which are offered for direct consumption. It also covers unshelled pistachios which are packed in bulk containers, and which are intended for repacking in consumer size containers.
sections.retrieval_text:
SCOPE
number=1
level=1
This standard applies to unshelled pistachios from varieties of Pistacia vera L. either in natural or in processed condition and which are offered for direct consumption. It also covers unshelled pistachios which are packed in bulk containers, and which are intended for repacking in consumer size containers.

page_spans.text: This standard applies to unshelled pistachios from varieties of Pistacia vera L. either in natural or in processed condition and which are offered for direct consumption. It also covers unshelled pistachios which are packed in bulk containers, and which are intended for repacking in consumer size containers.

sections.title: Product definition
sections.text: Pistachios are the product obtained from mature seeds from the fruit of Pistacia vera L. which have been artificially sun-dried and naturally or mechanically opened. The product may be roasted, salted, and/or lime-juice treated.
sections.retrieval_text:

number=2.1
level=2

sections.title: Lot acceptance
sections.text:
A lot will be considered as meeting the quality criteria requirements of the standard when: 
(a) there is no evidence of live infestation; and 
(b) the subsamples as taken in accordance with an appropriate sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5, (see relevant Codex texts on methods of analysis and sampling), meet the general requirements for subsections 3.3.1 and 3.3.2 and do not exceed the allowances for the respective defects in subsection 3.3.4.

sections.title: Quality factors – general requirements
sections.text:
(a) Practically free from mould and mouldy or rancid taste. 
(b) Free from living insects and mites. 
(c) Practically free from foreign matter – anything other than pistachio (kernel, hard shell and pericarp).

sections.title: Allowances for defects
sections.text:
The maximum allowances by count for defects as defined in Section 3.3.3 are as follows: 
 Category 3.3.3(a) – 5% 
 Category 3.3.3(b) -–5% 
 Category 3.3.3(c) – 8% 
 Category 3.3.3(d) – 4% 
 Category 3.3.3(e) – 1% 
Total defects (a) to (e) shall not exceed 10%.

sentences.original:
A lot will be considered as meeting the quality criteria requirements of the standard when: 
(a) there is no evidence of live infestation; and 
(b) the subsamples as taken in accordance with an appropriate sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5, (see relevant Codex texts on methods of analysis and sampling), meet the general requirements for subsections 3.3.1 and 3.3.2 and do not exceed the allowances for the respective defects in subsection 3.3.4.

classification.sentence:
A lot will be considered as meeting the quality criteria requirements of the standard when: 
(a) there is no evidence of live infestation; and 
(b) the subsamples as taken in accordance with an appropriate sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5, (see relevant Codex texts on methods of analysis and sampling), meet the general requirements for subsections 3.3.1 and 3.3.2 and do not exceed the allowances for the respective defects in subsection 3.3.4.
classification.pattern: Subject + will be considered as + condition clauses (a) and (b)

components.subject: A lot
components.condition_b: subsamples meet general requirements and do not exceed defect allowances

classification.justification: Complex conditional sentence defining compliance criteria; adapted pattern classification due to length and nested conditions.
notes: Incorporates references to external sampling standards.
Multiple conjunctive conditions.

sentences.original:
The maximum allowances by count for defects as defined in Section 3.3.3 are as follows: 
 Category 3.3.3(a) – 5% 
 Category 3.3.3(b) -–5% 
 Category 3.3.3(c) – 8% 
 Category 3.3.3(d) – 4% 
 Category 3.3.3(e) – 1% 
Total defects (a) to (e) shall not exceed 10%.

classification.sentence:
The maximum allowances by count for defects as defined in Section 3.3.3 are as follows: 
 Category 3.3.3(a) – 5% 
 Category 3.3.3(b) -–5% 
 Category 3.3.3(c) – 8% 
 Category 3.3.3(d) – 4% 
 Category 3.3.3(e) – 1% 
Total defects (a) to (e) shall not exceed 10%.
classification.pattern: Introductory phrase + enumeration of quantitative limits + total limit statement
classification.justification: Quantitative limits enumerated with references and total ceiling; matches limits and targets pattern with adaptation for complex enumeration.
notes: Includes total limit statement.
Percentage values vary per defect type.

notes: The use of 'should' here conveys a normative expectation often treated as obligation in regulatory text.
No exact template was found for 'should be in accordance with' phrasing referencing a standard.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR UNSHELLED PISTACHIO NUTS
pages.summary: A página contém título principal do documento e cabeçalho, sem seções ou texto estrutural adicional para mapear.

A página contém cabeçalho repetitivo no topo e texto de corpo sem títulos estruturais numerados ou em caixa alta que indicariam seções. Portanto, não há hierarquia estrutural detectada para mapeamento RAG nesta página.

A página contém um cabeçalho repetitivo 'CXS 131-1981 3'. A estrutura hierárquica principal foi identificada com 3 seções de nível 1 numeradas (1, 2, 3). A seção 2 contém subtítulos numerados de nível 2 (2.1 a 2.5) com textos descritivos associados. A seção 3 se subdivide em níveis 2 e 3 (até 3.3.1). Algumas seções terminam na página, outras indicam continuidade para a próxima página (por exemplo, 3.3.1). O texto está corretamente associado a cada nó e títulos são exibidos na página conforme presentes.

A página apresenta seções hierarquicamente organizadas a partir da seção 3.3.2 até a seção 6. As subseções 3.3.2, 3.3.3 e 3.3.4 continuam de página anterior, indicadas por is_continuation_from_previous_page=true. Os tópicos 3.4, 4, 5 e 6 iniciam na página atual. A seção 5 possui subtítulos numerados e títulos longos representando recomendações específicas, com texto associado quando disponível. O cabeçalho repetitivo foi identificado e não faz parte dos textos das seções. A hierarquia está adequada para uso em RAG, preservando numeração, títulos e textos pertinentes.

A página contém as seções 7 e 8 do documento CXS 131-1981. A seção 7 LABELLING é subdividida em 7.1 e 7.2, com 7.1 detalhada em três subseções: 7.1.1, 7.1.2 e 7.1.3. O texto relevante está associado corretamente a cada título. A seção 8 é apresentada como nível 1 com seu texto. Não há indicações de continuação de texto de ou para outras páginas. O cabeçalho contém o código do documento e a numeração da página, sendo excluído do texto interno para RAG.
